task_completion.description: Open the Subsurface dive log application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the target dive log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar or application interface indicates that '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf' is the currently open file.
The agent clicks on 'Log' -> 'Auto group dives' in the menu bar, or a similar automated grouping command is executed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the dives have been grouped into trips.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive list panel shows dives nested underneath 'Trip' headers (e.g., 'Trip to...'). There should be at least 2 distinct trips visible.

task_completion.description: Save the updated dive log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'File' -> 'Save', clicks a Save icon, or the title bar shows no unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename) after the grouping is complete.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the dive log application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface GUI, rather than terminal commands or a text editor modifying the .ssrf XML file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the automated grouping feature instead of manual grouping.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the 'Auto group dives' menu option, rather than manually creating trips and dragging individual dives into them.

integrity.description: The file was saved to the original target path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action overwrites the existing file at '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf' without using 'Save As' to create a new file elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf. When the Auto-Group feature is used, the 27 dives in the file will be organized into 4 distinct trips. The VLM should look for at least 2 trips (the minimum requirement) to verify successful grouping. Ignore the task description's claim of '8 expected grouped dives' as the actual file contains 27 dives.
